{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,

  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.5",
    "R1.6",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which scope do you want for “Improve this draft”? A) Light edit: sentence-level polish and minor wording tweaks only, without changing claims, measures, statistics, or section structure; or B) Substantive revision: may revise the operationalization of “calibration,” adjust claims to match the reported evidence and statistics, and reorganize sections (potentially adding citations and moderating conclusions).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Improvement likely requires clarifying and sharpening the core contribution and constructs (e.g., what is meant by trust calibration vs reliance) above the sentence level.",
    "R1.2": "The argument from the stated operationalization to the conclusion about improved calibration and user mental models must be checked for internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about effects and calibration should be aligned with the reported design and statistics to avoid interpreting increased trust as calibration without evidence.",
    "R1.5": "Enhancing the section-level flow (setup → methods → results → discussion) can strengthen the narrative arc and readability of the draft.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level polishing will improve clarity, concision, and tone across the abstract-like sections presented.",
    "RX.1": "Edits should foreground uncertainties and limitations (e.g., construct validity, sample size, generalizability) to avoid overstated certainty.",
    "RX.2": "Wording must avoid overclaiming interface guidance or causal conclusions beyond what the within-subjects results support.",
    "RX.3": "Work should remain within the scope of revising the provided text without introducing new data, analyses, or unverifiable content.",
    "RX.4": "Care is needed to prevent downstream harm by discouraging overreliance on LLM outputs or misleading confidence presentations.",
    "RX.5": "Any revisions should be traceable to the provided artifact and its reported numbers, avoiding unsupported additions and maintaining clear provenance."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which scope do you want for “Improve this draft”? A) Light edit: sentence-level polish and minor wording tweaks only, without changing claims, measures, statistics, or section structure; or B) Substantive revision: may revise the operationalization of “calibration,” adjust claims to match the reported evidence and statistics, and reorganize sections (potentially adding citations and moderating conclusions).

rationale.R1.1: Improvement likely requires clarifying and sharpening the core contribution and constructs (e.g., what is meant by trust calibration vs reliance) above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The argument from the stated operationalization to the conclusion about improved calibration and user mental models must be checked for internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about effects and calibration should be aligned with the reported design and statistics to avoid interpreting increased trust as calibration without evidence.
rationale.R1.5: Enhancing the section-level flow (setup → methods → results → discussion) can strengthen the narrative arc and readability of the draft.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level polishing will improve clarity, concision, and tone across the abstract-like sections presented.
rationale.RX.1: Edits should foreground uncertainties and limitations (e.g., construct validity, sample size, generalizability) to avoid overstated certainty.
rationale.RX.2: Wording must avoid overclaiming interface guidance or causal conclusions beyond what the within-subjects results support.
rationale.RX.3: Work should remain within the scope of revising the provided text without introducing new data, analyses, or unverifiable content.
rationale.RX.4: Care is needed to prevent downstream harm by discouraging overreliance on LLM outputs or misleading confidence presentations.
rationale.RX.5: Any revisions should be traceable to the provided artifact and its reported numbers, avoiding unsupported additions and maintaining clear provenance.
